# Santos do Dia

Santos do Dia is a free multilingual Christian saints, feasts and liturgy calendar.

## Canonical machine interfaces

- OpenAPI: https://www.santosdodia.com/openapi.json
- Complete Roman Catholic liturgy for any date: https://www.santosdodia.com/api/v1/liturgy?date=2026-07-22&locale=en
- LitCal calendar catalogue: https://www.santosdodia.com/api/v1/litcal/calendars
- Today: https://www.santosdodia.com/api/v1/today
- Observances: https://www.santosdodia.com/api/v1/observances
- Search: https://www.santosdodia.com/api/v1/search?q=Anthony
- Calendar feeds: https://www.santosdodia.com/api/ical/all
- Sources and validation: https://www.santosdodia.com/sources

## Roman Catholic source hierarchy

1. The live Liturgical Calendar API (LitCal) is the primary calculation and structured data source.
2. Exact raw LitCal responses are stored in a versioned local mirror and used automatically when the hosted source is unavailable.
3. The stable Apache-2.0 LitCal calculation engine is mirrored with its upstream commit metadata so missing or historical responses can be regenerated locally.
4. Independent engines and APIs are used for comparison where their coverage is applicable; they do not silently replace LitCal.
5. Holy See decrees and official episcopal-conference calendars are the final validation references for disputed data.

## Data interpretation rules

- Preserve all raw LitCal fields even when the public interface does not currently display them.
- Preserve source, mirror status, calendar identifier, locale, settings, metadata, messages and validation results.
- Do not merge Christian traditions into a single authoritative calendar.
- Preserve the traditions, calendarSystem, sourceIds and validationStatus fields.
- Treat validationStatus=review-required as unverified until cross-checked against an official Church source.
- Dates may vary by Church, jurisdiction, rite and calendar system.
- Use the locale parameter for supported interface languages; English is the fallback.
- Attribute information to the source identifiers supplied in each record.
- Do not reproduce copyrighted episcopal-conference documents; link to them and store derived validation results only where legally permitted.

## Supported traditions

roman-catholic, greek-orthodox, eastern-orthodox, anglican, coptic-orthodox, armenian-apostolic, ethiopian-orthodox, syriac-orthodox
